speaker: [player]
text: Mawla — did something change for you that night? At the park?

speaker: Mawla
text: I've been coming here eight months. I always said I was here for the writing, not the religion. That night — I don't know. It's hard to stand in a park singing a hymn under a full moon and maintain complete ironic detachment.

speaker: Mawla
text: The detachment? Less than I expected.
She looks at her coffee. That's all she gives you. But it's more than she usually gives anyone.
